introduction: “Physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration – even if slightly – of the vulva or anus, using a penis, other body parts or an object” (WHO 2003). A growing social disorder afflicting the poor and rich, alike, with devastating and longstanding emotional consequences for the afflicted, family and society at large.

features: Refusal to respond to simple questions

investigations: Vaginal/perineal swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivity
Urinalysis; urine microscopy, culture and sensitivity

goals: Assess and deal with the risk of sexually transmitted infections and pregnancy
It is important to document clinical findings
drug: Treat HIV infection (if detected); Post-exposure prophylaxis if clinical situation so requires
If seen within 120 hours, give emergency contraceptives (e.g. 1 tablet equivalent to 1.5 mg of levonorgestrel) to prevent unwanted pregnancy
Prevention of tetanus in certain situations

Rape is an indication for safe termination of pregnancy in some countries. If indeed, it is adjudged to threaten the life of the woman, this could also be a justification for termination of pregnancy in Nigeria

prevention: Legislate for new laws to deter potential rapists and protect females
Legislate for review to abortion law to allow for termination of pregnancy in case of rape
